text: The presence of the whole crew was required before the lunar rehearsal could begin. Mission control had chosen to automate much of the launch sequence, yet a human hand would still steer the craft at the critical moment. The pilot, pale with fatigue, adjusted her posture in the seat and felt a tremble run through the hull as the engines built thrust. "Stay accurate," the flight director said, his voice telling and calm. "One error could negate a year of work." An array of screens glowed nearby; on each, a linear graph traced the rocket's position. The simulate program would stimulate every fault the engineers could imagine, from a toxic fuel leak to a collide warning when two objects drew near in orbit. Nobody wanted a scandal, and nobody wanted to perish. The impetus for this mission had begun with a single vision: to place a small array of sensors on the virgin surface of a distant crater. Critics called the budget exaggeration, an ostentation of national pride, but approval had come at last after a long pending review. The team refused to let any flaw go unaddressed; they would rectify each one, thoroughly, before the real flight. In the pause, the pilot let her mind jog back to her childhood. She would slumber under a quilt while her father, a sympathetic man, told her of caravans crossing the desert, of a tuck of stars near the shore, of the lunar light that made the pepper plants in their garden gleam. He had a counterfeit pocket watch, bought cheap, yet to her it was a treasure. He would alight from his old motorcar, wail a funny tune, and tuck her in. That tendency to dream had steered her whole life. Now, on the simulator, an alarm cried out. A frail line on the screen turned red — an eruption of pressure in tank six. Her refusal to panic saved the run. She did not waver. With a quick thrust of the control stick she corrected the array, and the linear path settled. The director gave a slow nod of approval. "Accomplishment," he said, "is mostly the refusal to quit." Outside, near a flood-control channel by the shore, an athlete jogged past, unaware of the drama within. A maid swept the steps of the old telegraph office; a roller of fresh paint dried on a nearby wall. The world went on, weak to the wonders overhead. But inside the dome, the crew felt the irony: to reach the cold and additional silence of space, they had to master every inconvenient detail on the ground. "Enough for tonight," the director said. "We have a clearer position now." The conversion from fear to confidence was nearly complete. As the lights dimmed, the pilot paused, intent, and let one private prayer rise. The lunar dream, once a frail puzzle, now felt feasible. A pair of technicians shut the hatch, and the simulated ship went dark, its vision banked for another day. With some perplexity, the crew gripped the rails tightly, unwilling to let fear perturb them. To shorten the wait, the director told a strategic joke about a fridge full of grape juice and a single cold shot of soda. It was a blessing to laugh. Temporal worries would deplete anyone; they had to make peace with delay. A note on the wall, printed with a stencil, listed the aerodynamics drills and the elimination of each fault. Outside, near a road of fresh bitumen, an artillery museum stood like a haven; a caravan of trucks passed, marked ex works for an overseas importer. One could surmise, even without a course in botany, that the team's quiet idiosyncrasy was patience. The agency would indemnify any loss and would not persecute a single honest error. A small daily allowance, and the promise to promote the steady ones, kept spirits high. Unable to sleep, the pilot watched an old movie about forty pioneers, and felt the heat of ambition return.
translation: 在月球演练开始之前，全体机组人员必须到场。任务控制中心选择将大部分发射程序自动化，但在关键时刻仍需人手来驾驶飞船。飞行员因疲惫而面色苍白，她在座位上调整姿势，感到当引擎积聚推力时一阵颤动穿过船体。"保持精确，"飞行指挥说，他的声音很有分量却镇定，"一个差错就可能抵消一年的工作。"附近一排屏幕发着光；每块屏上，一条线性的曲线图描绘着火箭的位置。模拟程序会激发工程师能想象到的每一种故障，从有毒燃料泄漏到两个物体在轨道上接近时的碰撞警报。没人想要丑闻，也没人想丧命。这次任务的推动力始于一个单纯的愿景：在一个遥远陨石坑未被触碰的表面上放置一小组传感器。批评者称这预算是夸张，是民族自豪感的炫耀，但在漫长的搁置审查之后，批准终于到来。团队拒绝让任何缺陷无人处理；他们要在真正飞行之前彻底纠正每一个。在间歇中，飞行员让思绪慢跑回童年。她会盖着被子沉睡，而她那富有同情心的父亲，给她讲述穿越沙漠的商队，讲述海岸边一簇星辰，讲述使他们园中辣椒植株闪亮的月光。他有一只廉价买来的假冒怀表，但对她而言那是珍宝。他会从旧汽车上下来，哀号般地哼一支滑稽的曲子，给她盖好被子。那种爱做梦的倾向引导了她的一生。如今，在模拟器上，一个警报响起。屏幕上一条脆弱的线变红了——六号燃料箱压力骤升。她拒绝惊慌，救了这次演练。她没有动摇。她迅速一推操纵杆，修正了阵列，线性的航线稳定了下来。指挥缓缓点头表示认可。"成就，"他说，"大多是拒绝放弃。"外面，海岸边一条防洪水道旁，一名运动员慢跑而过，对这内部的戏剧浑然不觉。一名女仆扫着旧电报局的台阶；附近墙上一滚新刷的油漆正在晾干。世界照常运转，对头顶的奇迹无力察觉。但在穹顶内，机组感到了讽刺：为了抵达太空那寒冷而额外的寂静，他们必须在地面上掌握每一个不便的细节。"今晚够了，"指挥说，"我们现在的处境更清晰了。"从恐惧到自信的转变几近完成。当灯光暗下时，飞行员停顿，神情专注，让一句私下的祈祷升起。那个月球之梦，曾是一个脆弱的谜题，如今感觉可行了。一对技术员关上舱门，模拟的飞船陷入黑暗，它的愿景为又一天而搁置。带着几分困惑，机组紧紧抓住栏杆，不愿让恐惧扰乱他们。为了缩短等待，指挥讲了一个有策略的笑话，说一台装满葡萄汁的冰箱和一杯冰苏打。能笑出来是一种福分。世俗的忧虑会耗尽任何人；他们必须与延误握手言和。墙上一张用蜡纸印出的便条，列着空气动力学演练和每个故障的排除。外面，靠近一条新铺沥青的路，一座炮兵博物馆像避难所一样矗立；一队卡车驶过，标注着为一位海外进口商交货的字样。即使不修一门植物学课程，人们也能推测，这支团队安静的特质就是耐心。代理机构会赔偿任何损失，绝不会因一个诚实的错误而迫害任何人。一小笔每日津贴，以及提拔稳健者的承诺，让士气高昂。飞行员无法入睡，看了一部讲述四十位拓荒者的老电影，感到雄心的热度重新回来。
